The subtribe Pleurothallidinae is further classified under the tribe Epidendroideae. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics among these plant species.

intro: Stelis taxis, also known as Stelis taxi, is a flowering plant species native to Central America, belonging to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stelis taxis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been reported to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Stelis taxis is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including coughs, colds, and fever.
